dynasty.explanation: a series of rulers or leaders who are all from the same family

yoke.explanation: something that connects two things together; or a symbol of being under control/rule. Here it means 'under its control'.

succession.explanation: the process by which one person takes the place of another, especially as a ruler

heir.explanation: a person who has the legal right to receive someone's property or title when that person dies

reign.explanation: to rule as a king or queen

prosperity.explanation: the state of being successful and having a lot of money

vassal.explanation: a person who was given land by a lord and who had to promise to fight for and be loyal to that lord

paramount.explanation: more important than anything else; supreme

declared.explanation: to announce something formally or officially

petition.explanation: a formal written request to someone in authority

contend.explanation: to compete against someone to win something

descendant.explanation: someone who is related to a person who lived a long time ago

withdraw.explanation: to stop taking part in something or to remove yourself from a situation

ally.explanation: a country or person who has agreed to help and support another

council.explanation: a group of people chosen to give advice or make rules

claim.explanation: to say that something is true or that you have a right to something

oath.explanation: a formal promise, especially one made in a court of law

treason.explanation: the crime of betraying your country, especially by helping its enemies

decree.explanation: an official order issued by a ruler or authority

conspiracy.explanation: a secret plan made by a group of people to do something harmful or illegal

summon.explanation: to order someone to come to a place

defy.explanation: to refuse to obey someone or something

royal.explanation: relating to a king or queen

throne.explanation: the special chair that a king or queen sits on; also used to mean the position of being king or queen

realm.explanation: a country ruled by a king or queen

siege.explanation: a military operation in which an army surrounds a place and tries to capture it

garrison.explanation: a group of soldiers living in and defending a particular place

heritage.explanation: the traditions, achievements, and beliefs that are part of a family or group's history

lineage.explanation: the series of families that someone comes from originally

alliance.explanation: an agreement between countries or groups to work together

banner.explanation: a long piece of cloth with a design or message on it

infant.explanation: a very young child, especially one who cannot walk or speak yet

breech.explanation: when a baby is positioned to be born with its bottom or feet first instead of its head

womb.explanation: the organ inside a woman's body where a baby grows

spectator.explanation: a person who watches an event, especially a sports event

tourney.explanation: a competition in which knights fight with weapons, popular in medieval times

lance.explanation: a long, thin weapon with a pointed end, used by knights on horseback

wanton.explanation: done without good reason or in a way that shows lack of control

marvel.explanation: something that causes wonder and admiration

spike.explanation: a thin, pointed piece of metal or other material

implore.explanation: to ask someone in a very emotional and serious way to do something

grievance.explanation: a feeling of having been treated unfairly

concede.explanation: to admit that something is true, often unwillingly

relinquish.explanation: to give up something, especially power or control

usurp.explanation: to take power or a position illegally or by force

convene.explanation: to bring people together for a meeting

rebellion.explanation: an organized attempt to change the government using violence

allegiance.explanation: loyalty to a leader, country, or belief

submission.explanation: the act of accepting someone else's power or authority

retreat.explanation: to move away from a situation or place, especially because you are in danger

truce.explanation: an agreement between enemies to stop fighting for a period of time

abduct.explanation: to take someone away illegally by force

ransom.explanation: the money demanded for the return of someone who has been taken

retribution.explanation: severe punishment for something very wrong

treachery.explanation: behaviour that is not loyal and involves hurting someone who trusted you

dispense.explanation: to give out something, especially in a planned way

consort.explanation: the husband or wife of a ruler

depose.explanation: to remove someone from a powerful position

tribunal.explanation: a type of court that has authority to judge a particular dispute

verdict.explanation: a decision made by a jury or judge in a court of law

commission.explanation: to officially ask someone to do a special piece of work

envoys.explanation: a person sent to represent their government in another country

accord.explanation: a formal agreement between two groups or countries

breach.explanation: the act of breaking a law, promise, or agreement

consent.explanation: permission given for something to happen

behold.explanation: to look at or see someone or something (old or literary use)

deem.explanation: to consider or judge something in a particular way

counsel.explanation: advice or guidance, especially given by a professional

dwell.explanation: to live somewhere, or to keep thinking about something

bestow.explanation: to give something valuable as a gift or honor

proclaim.explanation: to announce something officially or publicly

tribute.explanation: something given or said to show respect or admiration

venture.explanation: a new activity that involves risk

valiant.explanation: showing great courage, especially in a difficult situation

despise.explanation: to hate someone or something very much

contempt.explanation: the feeling that someone or something is worthless or unimportant

console.explanation: to give comfort to someone who is sad or disappointed

indignation.explanation: anger about something that seems unfair or wrong

trepidation.explanation: a feeling of fear or anxiety about something that will happen

reckless.explanation: doing something dangerous without thinking about the risks

tumult.explanation: a state of confusion, noise, and excitement

peril.explanation: great danger, especially of being harmed or killed

formidable.explanation: very powerful and impressive, often causing fear or respect

fathom.explanation: to understand something after thinking about it carefully

discern.explanation: to see, notice, or understand something that is not obvious

abate.explanation: to become less strong or intense

reconcile.explanation: to restore friendly relations between people

condone.explanation: to accept or allow behavior that is morally wrong

intricate.explanation: having many small parts or details that are arranged in a complicated way

precede.explanation: to happen or exist before something else

prevail.explanation: to win or succeed after a struggle

respite.explanation: a short period of rest from something difficult or unpleasant

malevolent.explanation: wanting to cause harm to others

tenure.explanation: the period of time when someone has a particular job or position

abdicate.explanation: to give up the position of being king or queen

resolute.explanation: determined and not willing to change

slay.explanation: to kill someone violently (old or literary use)

foretell.explanation: to say what will happen in the future

dread.explanation: a strong feeling of fear about something that might happen

legacy.explanation: something that is left behind after someone dies, especially important achievements

potential.explanation: the possibility of becoming something greater in the future

charge.explanation: to rush forward to attack; or the price for something; or a responsibility

steadfast.explanation: not changing in your beliefs or loyalty

endure.explanation: to suffer something difficult without giving up

abound.explanation: to exist in large numbers or amounts

diminish.explanation: to become smaller or less important

forge.explanation: to shape metal by heating and hammering; to create something strong

grave.explanation: very serious and worrying; or a place where a dead person is buried

summit.explanation: the highest point of a mountain; or an important meeting between leaders
